title: Master-Detail, Data Surface and Long-Screen Governance

intent.problem: P0 queue, review and governance screens must not solve master/detail selection, table density, proof placement and long-screen debt locally.
intent.goal: Make the shared data-surface and master-detail contract enforceable before later domain epics migrate additional screens.
intent.principle: A visual workbench is not process completion. Selection, detail context, proof placement, command authority and long-screen exceptions must be explicit and testable.

targetScreens.reason: Advisor-facing review surfaces need typed selected context, one command zone and no advice/release overclaim.
Extraction review needs selected document context, proof drawer handoff and command-handoff semantics.
Consultant work intake needs scannable queue rows and a selected-client detail pane.
Advisor approval queue needs selected package context without implying approval from navigation.
Compliance release intake needs route handoff, blocker proof and proof drawer placement.
Evidence vault review needs selected evidence context while evidence visibility remains separate from sufficiency.
Governance user review needs permission/audit context without permission mutation overclaim.
Access request detail needs recovery context and explicit mutation gates.

masterDetailSurface: Queue or governance screens with one selected object and a separate selected-detail pane.
Board or table surfaces that need detail context without treating row selection as workflow completion.
Long P0 screens that can reduce vertical stacking by moving audit/proof history into a drawer or secondary tab.
dataSurface: Tables, lists, queues, boards, audit logs, redaction tables, permission matrices and detail-support lists.
Dense rows with explicit field priorities and action policies.
Disabled or static controls when the surface exposes why action is unavailable.
longScreenGovernance: Use master-detail, stepper, tabs, drawer extraction or approved route split before accepting a long P0 route.
Declare a machine-readable long-screen exception only as a debt ledger entry with owner, reason, expiry and follow-up task.
Keep command zones above proof appendices by moving history/provenance into approved proof slots.

masterDetailSurface: Using selected row state as approval, release, export, evidence sufficiency, permission mutation or client acceptance.
Rendering a full audit timeline as a permanent vertical appendix below every queue row/detail pair.
Using multiple primary command rails inside the same productive P0 workbench.
dataSurface: Hiding unavailable workflow authority behind enabled-looking row actions.
Treating sort/filter/search as process progress or gate completion.
Creating local one-off density, action or filter-state vocabularies for P0 screens.
longScreenGovernance: Calling a long screen accepted because it looks visually organized.
Using screenshots alone as process proof.
Adding a long-screen exception without owner, reason, expiry condition and follow-up ticket.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: The typed data-surface contract exposes canonical families, densities, action policies, filter states, master-detail modes and no-overclaim metadata.
MasterDetailSurface projects required queue workbench and selected-object runtime attributes.
At least one representative target screen consumes the shared EPIC-04 governance contract instead of a local stacked list/detail/audit fallback.
Long-screen exceptions remain explicit debt ledger entries and are not accepted by visual presence alone.
No row, queue, board or governance selection can imply release, export, evidence sufficiency, permission mutation, client visibility or client acceptance.

decisionBoundaries.rule: If a target screen cannot meet long-screen governance with a shared workbench, stepper, tab or drawer extraction, stop for a True-UX route-evolution decision.
This contract does not authorize blind schema replacement or new production API semantics.
This contract does not authorize unapproved advice, advisor-approval-as-release or export without compliance release/redaction.
